description.value:
Source: Ariadne's Book of Legends - Alpha v1.2, p. 44-49.

Legendary Fighter

The hard, practical mind of the Legendary Fighter is not something easily bought. In battle, loss is inevitable, and those who live for it tend to die for it sooner rather than later. A Legendary Fighter, be it for their drive, intelligence, talent, or sheer physical prowess, has thrived in that hell, and remains alive against brutally unfavorable odds–the chance of death in each crossing of swords, multiplied by ten thousand skirmishes. Their deeds paint their prowess as unparalleled, heroes of legend that snatch victory from the jaws of defeat time and time again. Yet for them, that martial capacity is but the result of a lifetime of training, of being knocked down, and of living to fight another day. This logic is what made them invincible in their own art.

The cosmos is full of beings that were born to power– Gods who emerged divine, mortals who seized the remnants of ancients, immortal creatures who accrued their strength across millennia of unchallenged existence. The Fighter was not born to it, and, had reason been followed, was destined for an unceremonious, early death. Every ability, every reflex, every scar was earned, one battle at a time–emerging victorious, or at least alive, against fate itself.

That is what beats at the hearts of Legendary Fighters, what commands their will and allows them to lead armies, conquer worlds, and slay Gods. In the end, no matter their accolades and storied accomplishments, they will always be that struggling soul, heaving against the world, crawling through the mud to get a ray of sun, and paying for it with blood and sweat. At 20th level, the Fighter has become the sum of every battle they have survived, distilled into something that operates at a level beyond technique, experience, and even logic. They act with a commitment and a depth of wisdom that no spell, no divine gift, and no cosmic inheritance can fully replicate. On a battlefield of Gods and monsters, the Legendary Fighter stands on equal ground. Their blade against the world, and the thrill of battle coming to their aid.

Bonus Features Legendary

20th +6 Perfect Strike, Legendary AC (Fighter), Legendary Resistance 1 4 6

Second Wind, Mythical Technique (1) 1 5 7

Level Proficiency

21st +6 Heroic Path (1), Legendary Score Improvement (1), Improved

22nd +7 Heroic Path (2), Mythical Technique (2), Perfect Defense,

Legendary Warrior 2 5 8

23rd +7 Heroic Path (3), Legendary Score Improvement (2), Legendary

Action, Mythical Technique (3) 2 6 9

24th +8 Heroic Path (4), Masterful Strike, Mythical Technique (4) 3 6 10

25th +8 Heroic Path (5), Legendary Score Improvement (3), Kensei

Technique 3 7 10

Level 20

Legendary Points

Starting at 20th level, you gain Legendary Points. Your level determines the number of points you have, as shown in the Legendary Points column of the Legendary Fighter table. You can take the Recover action on your turn to regain all expended Legendary Points. You can spend these points to fuel various features.

Legendary Resistance

Starting at 20th level, you can expend 1 Legendary Point when you fail a saving throw to succeed instead.

Legendary AC

At 20th level, if your AC calculation is lower than your level, you can use your level as your AC instead. If you are using your Legendary AC, you cannot further increase your AC in any way, except by temporary AC increases or magical effects lasting 1 hour or less. Other spells and effects that increase your AC only affect your regular AC.

Fighter’s AC. Increase your AC by 3. This bonus is increased by 1 when you reach 22nd and 24th level.

Perfect Strike

When you reach 20th level, you learn to channel your martial prowess into a single, devastating blow. When you take the Attack action, you can forgo any number of your attacks and expend 1 Legendary Point to perform a Perfect Strike. The damage calculation of Perfect Strike changes depending on the number of attacks forgone and the weapon type used (melee or ranged).

Second

Weapon Mastery

Points

Wind

Make a weapon attack against a target within range. This attack has an additional +10 bonus to hit and does not benefit from any other bonus to its damage roll. If you use your Action Surge on the same turn as a Perfect Strike, you can forgo any attacks granted by Action Surge to further increase the damage calculation, up to a maximum of 10 forgone attacks. Attacks made with a bonus action, such as an off-hand attack, cannot be forgone to increase Perfect Strike’s damage calculation.

Perfect Strike Damage

Attacks Forgone Melee Damage Ranged Damage

1 1d8 x 5 1d6 x 5

2 2d8 x 5 2d6 x 5

3 3d8 x 5 3d6 x 5

4 4d8 x 5 4d6 x 5

5 5d8 x 5 5d6 x 5

6 6d8 x 5 6d6 x 5

7 7d8 x 5 7d6 x 5

8 8d8 x 5 8d6 x 5

9 9d8 x 5 9d6 x 5

10 10d8 x 5 10d6 x 5

When you score a critical hit with your Perfect Strike, you do not multiply the damage by 2, but instead add 2d8 (melee) or 2d6 (ranged) to the equation.

Weapon Attack. Perfect Strike counts as a weapon attack and can be modified by or apply additional effects such as Weapon Masteries, Maneuvers, and Mythical Technique Infusions. Effects that add damage to a weapon attack do not apply.

Level 21

Heroic Path Advancement

At 21st level, you choose a Heroic Path you meet the prerequisites for and advance its first step. Each time you gain a level, you can advance a Heroic Path by one step, start a new Heroic Path, or, if specified as an option of the current step you’re on, jump from one Heroic Path into another.

Legendary Score Improvement

When you reach 21st level, and again at levels 23 and 25, increase one of your ability scores by 2 or two ability scores by 1. You cannot raise an ability score above 24 using this feature, unless another feature raised your maximum for that specific ability score beforehand.

Mythical Technique

At 21st level, you learn a Mythical Technique from the Mythical Techniques section. You learn an additional Mythical Technique when you reach 22nd, 23rd, and 24th levels.

5.5e: Whenever you learn a Mythical Technique, you gain an additional Weapon Mastery.

Improved Second Wind

At 21st level, you gain an additional use of your Second Wind feature. Whenever you use your Second Wind feature to heal, you gain temporary hit points equal to 1d10 + your fighter level.

You gain an additional charge of Second Wind at 23rd and 25th levels.

Level 22

Perfect Defense

Beginning at 22nd level, as long as you are wielding a weapon, you reduce the damage from weapon attacks by 10. In addition, whenever you are subject to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed and only half damage if you fail.

Legendary Warrior

Starting at 22nd level, you have learned the ways of battle and have proven yourself a legendary warrior. You learn 2 additional fighting styles from any class. You cannot be disarmed or forced to drop your weapon, and you become immune to the frightened condition.

Level 23

Legendary Action

At 23rd level, you can expend 1 Legendary Point to take an action at the end of another creature’s turn. You cannot take the Recover action in this way. If you take the Attack action, you can make up to 3 attacks. Once you use your Legendary Action, you cannot do so again until the end of your next turn.

Level 24

Masterful Strike

When you reach 24th level, your Perfect Strike dice categories are increased. If you are using a melee weapon, d8s are replaced by d12s. If you are using a ranged weapon, d6s are replaced by d10s.

Powerful Reserve

Also at 24th level, you can push your limits even when there is nothing more to give. If you have 0 Legendary Points, you can still use a feature that costs 1 Legendary Point. If you do so, the next time you use the Recover action, it does not restore any Legendary Points.

Level 25

Kensei Technique

At 25th level, you learn one Kensei Technique–a more powerful version of a Mythical Technique. Kensei Techniques follow the same rules as Mythical Techniques, as detailed in Part III, with one exception: once you use a Kensei Technique, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

The following techniques are available exclusively to Legendary Fighters. Each represents the absolute pinnacle of a particular martial school. A Fighter who learns a Kensei Technique has gone beyond what the school was designed to

teach. Kensei Techniques follow all the same rules as Mythical Techniques, but can only be used once per long rest,

Kensei Techniques

The School of Mastery

regardless of their LP cost.

Death of the Soul

Strike, Ichimonji

Classes: Fighter Channeling Time: 1 action Range: Weapon range, up to 60 feet Duration: Instantaneous LP Cost: 3 LP

You channel your soul into your weapon, releasing twin bursts of white flame. Make two weapon attacks against either one target or two separate targets within range.

Soul Strike. On a hit, the attack deals Soul damage instead of its normal damage type and the target’s soul is expelled from its body, casting it into the Ethereal Plane. The body falls unconscious and cannot be awakened while the soul remains separated.

The expelled soul appears within 5 feet of its body on the Ethereal Plane, retaining the creature’s full statistics. It can act on each of its turns but is confined to the Ethereal Plane and can only affect creatures and objects there. At the start of each of its turns, the soul can attempt to return to its body by succeeding on a Charisma saving throw against your Mythical Technique DC. On a success, the soul returns and the creature regains consciousness. Damage dealt to the soul is also dealt to the body, but damage dealt to the body does not affect the soul. When body and soul are reunited, the creature’s hit points become whichever of the two counts is lower. If the body is destroyed before the soul returns, the soul is lost in the Ethereal and cannot return unless the body is resurrected. If neither body nor soul has been destroyed after 1 minute, the soul snaps back automatically.

Creatures with no soul–such as certain Constructs and Undead–are unaffected by this technique. This technique can hit a creature in the ethereal plane.

Double Strike. If both attacks target the same creature and both hit, that creature must make a Charisma saving throw against your Mythical Technique DC. On a failure, its soul is not merely expelled–it is destroyed entirely. The creature dies instantly. Nothing short of a Wish spell cast at 10th level can restore a soul destroyed in this way.

Kensei’s Justice

Strike, Ichimonji

Classes: Fighter Channeling Time: 1 action Range: Unlimited Duration: Concentration, up to 1 minute LP Cost: 2 LP

You channel your soul into a powerful and inevitable strike, marking a creature within range. For the duration, whenever the marked target takes an action, bonus action, or Extra Action, you can use your reaction to teleport to an unoccupied space within 60 feet of it, regardless of the distance between you–even across planes of existence. When you teleport in this way, you can make a single attack against the target with advantage.

On a hit, the target takes an additional 110 (20d10) Soul damage and must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your Mythical Technique save DC or be stunned until the end of its next turn. If the target is immune to the stunned condition, it is impaired instead.

The effect ends early if the target dies.

Imprisonment. If this attack reduces the target to 0 hit points, its soul becomes bound inside your weapon as per the Imprisonment spell, to be released upon a condition of your choosing.

Rokkaku’s Sixteen Strikes

Strike, Zulfiqar

Classes: Fighter Channeling Time: 1 action Range: Weapon range or reach Duration: Instantaneous LP Cost: 2 LP

You infuse your weapon with a phantom echo of your own fighting spirit, and in a single devastating moment, every strike you could possibly deliver is realized at once.

As an action, you perform up to 16 special weapon attacks against targets within range. On a hit, each strike deals 20 Lethal damage.

These attacks do not benefit from Mythical Technique Infusions. On a critical hit, the damage for an attack is not doubled, but rather increases to 25. If the creature is bloodied and Lethal damage takes effect, the damage is not tripled; each critical strike deals 30 damage instead. These attacks do not benefit from any other bonus to their damage.

You can divide these attacks freely among any number of targets within range.

If you perform this technique with a ranged weapon, the maximum number of attacks is reduced to 12.

Greater Lotus Barrier

Strike, Ryuushinkan

Classes: Fighter Channeling Time: 1 reaction, which you take when you or a creature within 30 feet of you takes damage Range: 30 feet Duration: 1 round LP Cost: 2 LP

You project a barrier of pure soul-energy that encompasses you and up to 7 allies within range, interposing itself between them and all harm. The barrier absorbs damage directed at any protected creature and can withstand up to 1,000 points of damage total before breaking. It lasts until the end of your next turn.

For the duration, each creature protected by the Lotus Barrier cannot be forcibly moved, grappled, knocked prone, or displaced. They are immune to any effect that would reduce them to 0 hit points or kill them outright. The barrier protects against the triggering damage, against any secondary effects that damage would cause–such as a poison or a magical condition– and against the effects of other Mythical Techniques, including other Kensei Techniques.

If the barrier is destroyed, any damage in excess of the barrier’s remaining threshold is dealt to the creature or creatures targeted by the source that destroyed it.

source.custom: Original en-US text from Alpha v1.2
